text: Entering in the chest, the pleural cavity was mostly free from any pleural effusion.
Some adhesions were identified between the lower lobe and the diaphragm.
After taking down all of them, we've exposed the pulmonary ligament that was resected all the way up to the inferior pulmonary vein, whereas a station 9R node was removed for pathology.
The inferior and superior pulmonary veins were then exposed after opening the ventral side of the hilum.
A venous branch for the middle lobe originating from the lower pulmonary vein was then recognized, encircled with a vessel loop and resected with a 45 mm vascular staper.
A middle lobe bronchus was then evident after clearing the surrounding tissue and circling with a lower and a cotton string the bronchus.
This was resected with a 45 millimeter stapler.
Immediately behind the bronchial stump, the medial branch of the middle lobe segmental pulmonary artery was evident.
After encircling it with a vessel loop, it was resected with a 45 vascular stabler.

Then, the anterior aspect of the major fissure was encircled with a vessel loop and opened with a 60 mm stapler, gaining an optimal view of the lateral pulmonary artery branch for the segment of the middle lobe.
This was then resected with a 45 vascular stapler.
The minor fissure was then resected with a 60 mm stapler, paying careful attention to the second small pulmonary vein branch for the middle lobe that is originating from the upper pulmonary vein.
This one was recognized and resected with a 45 mm vascular stapler.
The specimen was then ready to be retrieved from the chest cavity with an endo bag.
The surgical procedure was therefore completed with a lymphonodal dissection of station 7 and station 2R and 4R.
